actionable_feedback: Provide explicit workbook cell-level citations in the memo (sheet name + cell references) for all packet-derived values and computed residuals so reviewers can map memo lines to workbook cells.

Produce an openpyxl-parsable export of the workbook (or an alternate machine-readable CSV/JSON for each sheet) so external validators can parse formulas and cell values. If environment constraints remain, include an OOXML-compatible, documented mapping file (sheet->cell->value/formula).

Add a machine-readable Validation_Checklist sheet (or separate artifact) that lists row-level checks with exact cell references, computed numeric results, pass/fail flags, and the stale threshold used. This should mirror the memo's table but be programmatically verifiable.

Clarify the semantic verification scope in the memo and in the deliverable manifest: explicitly state which items were validated via memo+trace only versus which would be validated only after providing a parsable workbook. Update the memo's language where the grader currently claims full semantic correctness to reflect the dependency on the parsable workbook.

Include a short README or final_message.txt describing the post-creation workbook patch (sheet modified, formulas changed) and list the exact formula edits made so auditors can quickly review the correction step recorded in the trace.
artifact_specific_comments: expert_memo.md: clear, reproduces numeric computations and flags ambiguities (missing spot/rate/expiry). To improve reproducibility, add exact sheet+cell citations for R1-R3 values and computed residuals.

options_parity_4.xlsx: created via a dependency-free OOXML write and later patched (trace step 8). Because the environment preview could not parse the XLSX, include a parsable export or per-sheet CSV/JSON to allow independent verification.
feedback_summary: Baseline deliverables are complete and internally consistent: memo reproduces row-level parity-proxy calculations and monotonicity checks and remediation scenarios are costed against budget. Two verifier warnings remain: (1) the workbook XLSX could not be parsed in the preview environment (openpyxl unavailable), preventing independent cell-level verification; (2) a minor inconsistency between deterministic precheck note ('semantic_correctness_not_evaluated') and the grader's semantic score should be clarified. Addressing these will improve reproducibility and verifier confidence.

extra_model_fields.evaluator_notes: Baseline earns full credit under the artifact-contract precheck. Addressing the parsing and clarity items will reduce residual verification risk and make results reproducible in independent environments.
